query: What are the air quality trends in the city?

response.headline: Reykjavík's Air Cleaner Than It Was 15 Years Ago
response.lede: If you're breathing the air in Reykjavík today, it's cleaner than it was decades ago. Since 1994, harmful concentrations in the air have dropped by about 75%, making the city a healthier place to live.

story_blocks.body: Concentrations of pollutants in the air have plummeted by 75% since 1994, reflecting a major improvement in air quality.

story_blocks.heading: Air Pollution Has Dropped Dramatically Since 1994
story_blocks.body: In the chart below, you'll see the steep decline in air pollution levels since 1994. This illustrates a significant decrease in harmful substances, from a peak level of 134 µg/m3 in 1994 down to just 34 µg/m3 by 2016. Such a drop highlights how much cleaner and safer Reykjavík's air has become.

story_blocks.heading: Comparing Different Air Pollutants
story_blocks.body: The bar chart below shows average readings of various pollutants over the years. Notably, ozone levels have increased over time, suggesting that while many pollutants decreased, not all trends are the same. Each bar represents a different substance, helping to identify which are most or least prevalent now.

story_blocks.heading: Tracking Changes Over the Seasons
story_blocks.body: The area chart provides insight into seasonal patterns of pollution levels. Some substances show noticeable spikes or drops during particular months, indicating that air quality varies not just over the years but throughout each year as well.

story_blocks.heading: What This Means for Residents
story_blocks.body: For the people of Reykjavík, these improvements mean breathing easier and living healthier lives. Fewer pollutants in the air reduce health risks and improve overall quality of life. However, ongoing attention is needed to ensure that rising ozone levels don't reverse this positive trend.

response.data_note: This data covers the period from 1994 to 2016, showing how air quality has changed over time. While most air pollutants have decreased, ozone levels have actually increased. It's important to keep in mind that this information is based on measurements from Grensásvegi station in Reykjavík. Data license details were not provided, so consider this when interpreting the numbers.
response.followup_question: How has water quality in local rivers changed over the years?
